meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer SaaS Pricing: Overcome No Design Skills (Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with design. Use this intermediate checklist to build professional SaaS pricing tables using Antigravity and Claude Code for fast delivery.
content.answer_block: Eliminate design bottlenecks by leveraging component-driven architecture. Use Antigravity to scaffold high-converting pricing layouts and Claude Code to inject dynamic logic. By treating pricing as a modular data structure rather than a visual design task, you ensure pixel-perfect, responsive results that clients love, without needing a dedicated UI designer.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking. Your client needs a SaaS pricing page that converts, but your design skills are stalling the project. You’re staring at a blank canvas, paralyzed by CSS Flexbox alignment and color theory, while your billable hours evaporate into the void of 'trying to make it look right.'

It doesn't have to be this way. The secret isn't learning to design; it's learning to engineer UI as a system. By shifting from manual pixel-pushing to structured, AI-assisted component generation, you can ship enterprise-grade pricing modules in minutes, not days. Let’s turn your technical debt into a competitive advantage.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Data Model
steps.description: Before touching CSS, structure your pricing tiers as a JSON object to decouple data from the view.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing table including tiers for Basic, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for name, price, currency, feature list, and CTA text.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate a pricing-data.ts file based on the standard SaaS tier structure.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Layout with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, accessible layout wrapper that enforces strict spacing and alignment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive Tailwind CSS pricing component. Use a 3-column grid, centered alignment, and professional white-space spacing. Ensure mobile-first responsiveness.

steps.title: Inject Dynamic Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Automate the rendering of pricing cards to avoid hardcoding repetitive HTML/JSX elements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React function that maps over the pricing data object and renders a card for each tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit pricing-component.tsx 'Replace static HTML cards with a .map() function iterating over pricing-data.ts'

steps.title: Implement Conversion-Focused Micro-interactions
steps.description: Add subtle hover states and focus indicators to increase user engagement without custom design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add Tailwind hover effects to the pricing cards, including a subtle scale-up transition and a primary button color shift.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /apply 'Add hover:scale-105 and transition-all to pricing card containers'

steps.title: Automated Testing for Responsiveness
steps.description: Verify your component across different breakpoints without manual browser resizing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright test script to check if the pricing component displays correctly on mobile (375px) and desktop (1440px).

pro_tips: Always prioritize high-contrast typography over complex iconography; it looks more professional and is easier to maintain.
Use 'CSS Variables' for your color palette so you can re-theme the entire pricing page for a client in seconds.
Include a 'Most Popular' badge with a slightly larger scale to guide user eyes, even if you lack design skills.
Avoid custom fonts; stick to system font stacks (Inter or system-ui) to ensure the fastest possible page load speeds.
Embed your pricing logic in a separate utility file to keep your view components thin and focused on layout only.
content.next_steps: Deploy your component to a staging environment, run a lighthouse audit to verify accessibility scores, and present the modular structure to your client as a 'design system' upgrade.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design limitations dictate your project velocity. Master the intersection of AI-assisted scaffolding and clean code to double your output. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
